back_translation: [[But Jesus went to the Mount of Olives.]]
[[At early dawn, He went back again to the temple courts. All the people came to Him, and He sat down and taught them.]]
[[Then the scribes and the Pharisees brought to Him a woman who had been caught while committing adultery, and they made her stand in the middle of the assembly.]]
[[Then they said to Him in order to test Him, “Teacher, this woman was caught red-handed while committing adultery.]]
[[In the Law, Moses commanded us to take stones and stone such a woman to death. So You — what do You say?”]]
[[They said this to test Him, and to find a basis to accuse Him. But Jesus bent His body down and used His finger to write on the ground, [pretending not to pay attention].]]
[[When they would not stop questioning Him, He straightened His body and said to them, “Whoever among you is without sin, let that one throw a stone at her first.”]]
[[Then He bent His body down again and wrote on the ground.]]
[[When they heard this, and their own conscience caused them to feel guilty, they went out one by one, beginning with the elders, leaving Jesus alone, with the woman still standing in the middle.]]
[[Then Jesus straightened His body, and when He looked and saw no one but the woman, He said to her, “Where are they—those who accused you? Has no one judged and condemned you?”]]
[[She said, “No one, my Lord.” Jesus said, “Then neither do I judge and condemn you. Go now, and do not sin again.”]]
Jesus spoke to them again, saying, “I am the light of the world. Whoever follows Me will not walk in darkness at all, but will have the light of life.”
Then the Pharisees said to Him, “You are testifying about yourself; your testimony is not true.”

Jesus answered and said to them, “Even if I testify about myself, my testimony is true, because I know where I came from and where I am going. But you do not know where I came from or where I am going.
You judge by the standards of the flesh; I judge no one at all.
But if I do judge, my judgment is just, because I am not alone, but the Father who sent Me is with Me.
And in your own Law it is written that the testimony of two men is true.
I am the one who testifies about myself, and the Father who sent Me also testifies about Me.”
Then they asked Him, “Where is your Father?” Jesus answered, “You know neither Me nor My Father. If you knew Me, you would also know My Father.”
He spoke these words while He was teaching near the treasury in the temple courts. But no one seized Him, because His appointed time had not yet come.
Then He said to them again, “I am going away. You will seek Me, and you will die in your sin. Where I am going, you cannot come.”
Then the Jewish leaders said among themselves, “Will he kill himself, since he says, ‘Where I am going, you cannot come’?”
Then He said to them, “You are from below; I am from above. You are of this world; I am not of this world.
Therefore I told you that you would die in your sins. For if you do not believe that I am, you will die in your sins.”
Then they asked Him, “Who are you?” Jesus said to them, “Just as I have been telling you from the beginning.
I have many things to say and to judge concerning you. But the One who sent Me is true, and what I have heard from Him, those things I speak to the world.”
They did not understand that He was speaking to them about the Father.
Jesus then said, “When you have lifted up the Son of Man, then you will know that I am, and that I do nothing on My own, but I speak these things just as the Father has taught Me.
And the One who sent Me is with Me. He has not left Me alone, because I always do what pleases Him.”
While He was saying these things, many believed in Him.
Then Jesus said to the Jews who had believed in Him, “If you abide in My word, you are truly My disciples,
and you will know the truth, and the truth will set you free.”
They answered Him, “We are descendants of Abraham, and have never been slaves to anyone. Why do you say, ‘You will be set free’?”
Jesus answered them, “Amen, amen, I tell you, everyone who commits sin is a slave of sin.
A slave does not remain in the house forever, but a son remains forever.
Therefore, if the Son sets you free, you will be truly free.
I know that you are descendants of Abraham, but you are trying to kill Me, because My word has no place in your heart.
I speak the things I have seen when I was with the Father, and you do the things you have heard from your father.”
They answered Him, “Our father is Abraham.” Jesus said to them, “If you were children of Abraham, you would do the works of Abraham.
But now you are trying to kill Me, who am a man who has told you the truth, the truth I heard from God. Abraham did not do this.
You do the works of your father.” Then they said to Him, “We were not born from adultery. We have one Father — God.”
Jesus said to them, “If God were your Father, you would love Me, because I came out from God and am here. I have not come on My own, but He sent Me.
Why do you not understand My words? Because you cannot truly receive and listen to My word.

You are from your father, the devil, and you desire to do the desires of your father. He has been a murderer of humans from the beginning, and does not stand in the truth, because there is no truth in him. When he speaks falsehood, he speaks according to his own nature, because he is a liar and the father of lies.
But because I speak the truth, you do not believe Me.
Among you, who is able to prove that I have sin? If I speak the truth, why do you not believe Me?
Whoever is from God listens to the words of God. The reason you do not listen is that you are not from God.”
Then the Jewish leaders answered Him, “When we say that you are a Samaritan and have a demon possessing you — we are right, are we not?”
Jesus answered, “I do not have a demon, but I give honor to My Father, and you dishonor Me.
I do not seek my own glory. There is One who seeks, and He is the Judge.
Amen, amen, I tell you, if anyone keeps My word, that one will never see death forever.”

Then the Jewish leaders said to Him, “Now we know for certain that you have a demon. Abraham died, and the prophets died. But you say, ‘If anyone keeps My word, that one will never taste death forever.’
Are you greater than our father Abraham? Abraham died, and the prophets also died. Who do you claim yourself to be?”
Jesus answered, “If I glorify Myself, My glory is worthless. The One who glorifies Me is My Father, the One whom you say, ‘He is our God,’
yet you do not know Him. But I know Him. If I were to say I do not know Him, I would be a liar like you. But I know Him, and I keep His word.
Abraham, your father, rejoiced exceedingly to see My day, and he saw it and was glad.”
Then the Jewish leaders said to Him, “You are not yet fifty years old, and have you seen Abraham?”
Jesus said to them, “Amen, amen, I tell you, before Abraham came to be, I am.”
Then they picked up stones to throw at Him. But Jesus hid Himself and went out from the temple area.
